description.en: Vienna's iconic breakfast roll, its crust folded by hand into a five-point star before baking. A long, cool preferment builds flavor in an otherwise lean dough, giving a crackling crust and a mild, airy crumb that toasts beautifully.
description.de: Wiens berühmtes Frühstücksbrötchen mit der von Hand gefalteten Fünf-Stern-Kruste. Ein langer, kühler Vorteig gibt dem sonst mageren Teig sein Aroma – reschen Glanz außen, helle, luftige Krume innen.
description.es: El icónico panecillo vienés de desayuno, con su corteza plegada a mano en forma de estrella de cinco puntas. Una larga prefermentación en frío aporta sabor a esta masa magra, dejando una corteza crujiente y una miga clara y ligera.
description.fr: Le petit pain viennois emblématique du petit-déjeuner, à la croûte pliée à la main en étoile à cinq pointes. Une longue préfermentation au frais développe l'arôme de cette pâte pourtant maigre, pour une croûte craquante et une mie claire et légère.

origin.note: A Viennese bakery classic since the 18th–19th century, since spread across the whole German-speaking world as a standard breakfast roll.

ingredients.note: for crust color and flavor; can be omitted

keeping.note: Best the day it's baked; the thin crust softens fast. Refresh in a hot oven a few minutes, or freeze same-day.

method.title: Build the preferment
method.summary: The evening before, stir flour, water and a little yeast into a loose, wet preferment. Cover and let it ripen about 12 hours at room temperature, this does most of the flavor-building work.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Combine the ripened preferment with the remaining flour, water, salt and yeast. Knead to a smooth, elastic dough, lean and firm, with no fat or sugar to soften it.

method.title: Bulk ferment with folds
method.summary: Let the dough rise about 90 minutes at room temperature, folding it firmly two or three times in the first half to strengthen the gluten and even out fermentation.

method.title: Divide and rest
method.summary: Divide into nine 90 g pieces, round each one, and let them rest twice, seam-side down, so the dough relaxes enough to shape without tearing.

method.title: Fold the star
method.summary: Flatten each piece in coarse rye flour and fold it by hand into the traditional five-lobed kaiser pattern, tucking each fold under the last so the top splits into points as it bakes.

method.title: Proof briefly
method.summary: Proof seam-side down on a floured cloth for 20–25 minutes, then flip right-side up onto the baking sheet and let the surface dry uncovered for 10 minutes, this is what gives the crust its shine and crackle.

method.title: Bake with steam
method.summary: Bake at high heat with a burst of steam, then vent and finish at a slightly lower temperature until deep gold, about 25 minutes total. Cool briefly before the first bite.

source.author: Lutz Geißler (style reference)
source.adaptation: Simplified from a professional 1931-style Polish-preferment formula; scaled to a home-bakeable overnight schedule, malt flour treated as optional.
